title: Laser lighting module with safety function
abstract: The invention describes a laser lighting module (100) for a vehicle headlight comprising at least one laser (110), a scanning arrangement (120), a light conversion device (130), a safety mirror (142), a safety detector (144) and a safety controller (146), wherein the at least one laser (110) is arranged to emit laser light (10) characterized by a first wavelength range, wherein the scanning arrangement (120) is arranged to move a laser beam of the laser light (10) within a scanning solid angle such that a laser spot of the laser light is moved across a light entrance surface of the light conversion device (130), wherein the light conversion device (130) comprises a light converter, wherein the light converter is arranged to convert a fraction of the laser light (10) to converted light (20), wherein the converted light (20) is characterized by a second wavelength range different than the first wavelength range, wherein the light conversion device (130) comprises a light emission surface arranged to emit a mixture of transmitted laser light (14) and converted light (20), wherein the safety mirror (142) is arranged within the scanning solid angle such that at least 90% of the transmitted laser light (14) emitted within the scanning solid angle hits the safety mirror (142), wherein the safety mirror (142) is arranged to provide safety detection light (16) by reflecting a fraction of the transmitted laser light (14) hitting the safety mirror (142) to the safety detector (144), wherein the safety detector (144) is arranged to generate, irrespective of any polarization of the safety detection light (16), at least one control signal (45) upon detection of the safety detection light (16), wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to receive the at least one control signal (45) from the safety detector (144), and wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to switch off the at least one laser (110) if the at least one control signal (45) exceeds a first threshold value (51). The invention further describes a vehicle headlight comprising such a laser lighting module (100).
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A laser lighting module (100) for a vehicle headlight comprising at least one laser (110), a scanning arrangement (120), a light conversion device (130), a safety mirror (142), a safety detector (144), and a safety controller (146), wherein the at least one laser (110) is arranged to emit laser light (10) characterized by a first wavelength range, wherein the scanning arrangement (120) is arranged to move a laser beam of the laser light (10) within a scanning solid angle such that a laser spot of the laser light is moved across a light entrance surface of the light conversion device (130), wherein the light conversion device (130) comprises a light converter, wherein the light converter is arranged to convert a fraction of the laser light (10) to converted light (20), wherein the converted light (20) is characterized by a second wavelength range different than the first wavelength range, wherein the light conversion device (130) comprises a light emission surface arranged to emit a mixture of transmitted laser light (14) and converted light (20), wherein the safety mirror (142) is arranged within the scanning solid angle such that at least 90% of the transmitted laser light (14) emitted within the scanning solid angle hits the safety mirror (142), wherein the safety mirror (142) is arranged to provide safety detection light (16) by reflecting a fraction of the transmitted laser light (14) hitting the safety mirror (142) to the safety detector (144), wherein the safety detector (144) is arranged to generate, irrespective of any polarization of the safety detection light (16), at least one control signal (45) upon detection of the safety detection light (16), wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to receive the at least one control signal (45) from the safety detector (144), and wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to switch off the at least one laser (110) if the at least one control signal (45) exceeds a first threshold value (51). 2. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1, wherein the safety mirror (142) is arranged between the light conversion device (130) and an optical module (150), wherein the optical module (150) is arranged to transform the transmitted laser light (14) and the converted light (20) for projecting the transmitted laser light (14) and the converted light (20) to an image plane. 3. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the light converter (130) is attached to a transparent element (140, 150), and wherein the safety mirror (142) is comprised by the transparent element (140, 150). 4. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 3, wherein the safety mirror (142) comprises at least a part of a surface or interface of the transparent element (140, 150). 5. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 4, the laser lighting module (100) further comprising an optical module (150) for transforming the transmitted laser light (14) and the converted light (20) for projecting the transmitted laser light (14) and the converted light (20) to an image plane, and wherein the part of the surface is comprised by an optical element of the optical module (150). 6. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the laser lighting module (100) comprises an optical concentrator (143), wherein the optical concentrator (143) is arranged to concentrate the safety detection light (16) to the safety detector (144). 7. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the safety detector (144) comprises an array of detector elements, and wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to receive a plurality of control signals from the detector elements. 8. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the safety detector (144) comprises one detector element, and wherein the safety mirror (142) or an optical concentrator (143) are arranged to concentrate the safety detection light (16) to the detector element. 9. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to switch off the at least one laser (110) if the control signal (45) falls below a second threshold value. 10. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the safety mirror (142) comprises an antireflective coating in the second wavelength range for reducing reflection of converted light (20) to the safety detector (144). 11. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the safety mirror (142) comprises a reflective coating which is reflective in the first wavelength range to provide the safety detection light (16). 12. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 11, wherein the reflective coating is arranged to reflect light in the first wavelength range received by the safety mirror (142) within the scanning solid angle under a solid angle smaller than 10Â° with respect to an emission direction of laser light (10, 12) provided by the scanning arrangement (120) and having been transmitted through the light conversion device (130) without having been scattered. 13. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the safety mirror (142) further comprises a reflective release structure, wherein the scanning arrangement (120) is arranged to direct the laser beam of the laser light (10), at the beginning of laser emission or in predefined time periods, to the release structure, wherein the reflectivity of the release structure or the scanning arrangement (120) are arranged that the safety detection light (16) reflected by the release structure are detectable in the safety detector (144), wherein the safety detector (144) is arranged to generate a release signal upon detection of the safety detection light (16) reflected by the release structure, wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to receive the release signal from the safety detector (144), and wherein the safety controller (146) is arranged to switch off the at least one laser (110) if the release signal is not received at the beginning of laser emission or in the predefined time periods. 14. The laser lighting module (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the safety detector (144) comprises an optical filter being transmissive in the first wavelength range and reducing transmission of light in the second wavelength range for reducing detection of converted light (20) by the safety detector (144). 15. A vehicle headlight comprising a laser lighting module (100) according to any one of claims 1 - 14 and an electrical interface, wherein the electrical interface is arranged to transfer electrical signals to the at least one laser (110).
